new_classes.label_en: Purification process quality (loses fermented beverage characteristics)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of an undenatured ethyl alcohol product that is obtained not exclusively through fermentation but also through a process of purification, which leads to the product losing the properties and characteristics of fermented beverages and acquiring those of ethyl alcohol.

new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength < 80% vol, obtained by fermentation and purification, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 90 99)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von weniger als 80 % vol, unvergällt, durch Gärung und Reinigung gewonnen, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l (KN 2208 90 99)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol, obtained not exclusively through fermentation but also through a process of purification causing it to lose the properties of fermented beverages, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) undenatured ethyl alcohol quality, (2) alcoholic strength less than 80% vol, (3) product obtained not exclusively through fermentation but also through a purification process that causes it to lose fermented beverage properties — modelled as a new PurificationProcessQuality, and (4) containers holding more than 2 litres (LargeContainer ≥ 2 L). A minor gap is that the exact upper bound of the container size (> 2 L) is captured only implicitly by using LargeContainer (≥ 2 L), which is close but technically admits exactly 2 L; the sibling code 22089091 covers containers ≤ 2 L. Additionally, the purification process aspect is modelled as a quality rather than as a process restriction, which is a slight ontological simplification. The legal note also does not explicitly state an upper container volume limit beyond 2 L for this code, so the LargeContainer class is the best available approximation.
